usageExample: The firefighters put out the kitchen fire quickly.

usageExample: The receptionist put me through to billing.
tip: Use this when you mean to connect by phone.

usageExample: Small businesses rely on repeat customers.
tip: Use this when you mean to depend on.

usageExample: The doctor ruled out an infection after the blood test.
tip: Use this when you mean to eliminate as a possibility.

usageExample: I ran across an interesting article about memory training.
tip: Use this when you mean to find unexpectedly.

usageExample: Can I run this idea by you before the meeting?
tip: Use this when you mean to ask for quick feedback.

usageExample: The meeting ran over by fifteen minutes.
tip: Use this when you mean to last longer than planned.

usageExample: They are saving up for a new apartment.
tip: Use this when you mean to gradually save money.

usageExample: I set aside thirty minutes each morning to read.
tip: Use this when you mean to reserve.

usageExample: The storm set back construction by a week.
tip: Use this when you mean to delay progress.

usageExample: The smoke set off the alarm.
tip: Use this when you mean to cause to start.

usageExample: They set out before sunrise to reach the summit.
tip: Use this when you mean to begin a journey.

usageExample: Panic set in when the lights went out.
tip: Use this when you mean to begin and continue.

usageExample: After years of traveling, he settled down in Portland.
tip: Use this when you mean to become calm or stable.

usageExample: He bought the car mainly to show off.
tip: Use this when you mean to try to impress.

usageExample: The factory shut down during the power outage.
tip: Use this when you mean to stop operating.

usageExample: I signed up for a beginner yoga class.
tip: Use this when you mean to register.

usageExample: Please slow down; I cannot write that fast.
tip: Use this when you mean to reduce speed.

usageExample: The contract spells out each partner's responsibilities.
tip: Use this when you mean to explain clearly.

usageExample: The technician is standing by in case the stream fails.
tip: Use this when you mean to wait ready or support.

usageExample: What does this abbreviation stand for?
tip: Use this when you mean to represent.

usageExample: Her application stood out because of her volunteer work.
tip: Use this when you mean to be noticeable.

usageExample: We stayed up late watching old movies.
tip: Use this when you mean to remain awake.

usageExample: Stick around after class if you have questions.
tip: Use this when you mean to stay for a while.

usageExample: Please switch off the lights before you leave.
tip: Use this when you mean to turn off.

usageExample: The assistant took down notes during the interview.
tip: Use this when you mean to write down or remove.

usageExample: He took apart the old radio to see how it worked.
tip: Use this when you mean to separate into pieces.

usageExample: I take back what I said about the plan being impossible.
tip: Use this when you mean to withdraw a statement.

usageExample: The museum was so large that we could not take it all in.
tip: Use this when you mean to absorb or understand.

usageExample: She took on extra work while her coworker was away.
tip: Use this when you mean to accept responsibility.

usageExample: He took up running after his doctor suggested exercise.
tip: Use this when you mean to start a hobby.

usageExample: Let us talk over the offer before we respond.
tip: Use this when you mean to discuss carefully.

usageExample: The city tore down the old parking garage last spring.
tip: Use this when you mean to demolish.

usageExample: The judge threw out the weak evidence.
tip: Use this when you mean to discard or reject.

usageExample: We tried out three apps before choosing one.
tip: Use this when you mean to test something.

usageExample: The side project turned into a full-time business.
tip: Use this when you mean to change into.

usageExample: We used up all the printer paper during training.
tip: Use this when you mean to use completely.

usageExample: Watch out for ice on the steps.
tip: Use this when you mean to be careful.

usageExample: Write down the address before you forget it.
tip: Use this when you mean to record in writing.

usageExample: The airline added on extra fees at checkout.
tip: Use this when you mean to include as an extra.
